name: replay_new_position_open_reset
description: Replay across a position-open boundary: when a new day's tick sequence starts (new position), prev_persisted_stop must be reset to None so the ratchet does not carry the prior day's stop level forward. Mirrors E2 AC-E2.5 (live path resets stop_trigger=None on position close).
provenance: schema-derived. No producer output hardcoded. The key assertion is structural: prev_persisted_stop_level=None on tick_idx=0 of a new day's tick sequence.

days.description: Day A runs to completion. The final stop level at day-end is some positive value (call it stop_day_A). The replay loop resets stop_state between days.
days.tick_sequence: 5 qualifying ticks -> lock fires -> stop reaches 1.8
days.end_of_day_stop_level: derived from replay, call it S_A (> 0)
days.note: S_A is a symbolic placeholder; the test asserts shape (is positive float), not value.

days.description: Day B starts fresh: new position, prev_persisted_stop must reset to None for tick_idx=0. The ratchet must NOT carry S_A into Day B's stop calculations.

tick_0_expected.note: Each day in autotuner replay represents a fresh position (the loop re-initialises stop_level state per day). Day B tick 0 must receive None, not S_A.

days.invariant: Day B's tick-0 stop computation is unconstrained by Day A's stop level.

assertion_contract.day_boundary_reset: prev_persisted_stop passed to cycle-0 of Day B is None (not Day A's terminal stop level)
assertion_contract.within_day_monotonicity: stop levels within Day B form a non-decreasing sequence once ratchet is threaded
assertion_contract.cross_day_independence: Day B's stop sequence starts from zero-basis, not clamped by Day A
